title: ChargePoint Q4 2024 Earnings: Revenue Miss and Growth Prospects
text: ChargePoint Holdings (NYSE:CHPT) faced headwinds in the fourth quarter of 2024. The company's revenue declined significantly, falling to $101.9 million, representing a 12% year-over-year decrease. Management's forward guidance proved cautious, projecting next-quarter revenues of $100 million, trailing analyst expectations by 1.3%. The firm reported a GAAP loss of $0.14 per share, which aligned with consensus estimates. Despite recent struggles, ChargePoint's historical trajectory shows promise. Over the preceding five years, the company achieved an 18.9% compound annual growth rate, outperforming industrial benchmarks. However, this momentum stalled, with revenues declining at a 5.6% annualized pace over the past two years, reflecting broader cyclical challenges in renewable energy sectors. Performance diverged significantly across business divisions. Hardware sales through Networked Charging Systems contracted by an average 12.2% annually, while the Subscriptions segment (software-related revenue) expanded robustly at 31.2% annually. This disparity highlights the company's shifting business composition. Analysts project recovery ahead, forecasting 15.9% revenue growth over the next 12 months. Improvements in operational efficiency and earnings per share (up 49.8% annually over five years) suggest the company is moving toward profitability, though continued challenges remain.
